rag_rules: Preserve the existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Place component type annexes in valid component type scopes
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes after subcomponents and connections
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
The authoritative source for component names, ports, directions, data types, subcomponents, and connections is the input AADL model
generic_rules: Maintain the existing model structure when inserting new annex blocks
Ensure new annex blocks are placed in valid scopes according to AADL and AGREE rules
Preserve the existing subcomponents and connections when inserting implementation-level bindings
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
Implementation annexes are for implementation-level bindings such as assignments
anti_patterns: Do not place assumptions or guarantees referring to component ports in implementation scopes
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants
